input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI development on a clean macOS machine.

---

## 1. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install the **latest stable Xcode** (currently Xcode 16.x).
- After install, run:
  ```
  sudo xcode-select --switch /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  ```
- Accept the license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version` should print the version and build number.

---

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — grab the latest **.NET 8 SDK** (or .NET 9 if you want preview MAUI features). Use the **macOS arm64** or **x64** installer as appropriate.
- **Discover versions:** The download page always shows the latest recommended SDK. For specific versions, check [dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/download/dotnet](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/download/dotnet).
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version` and `dotnet --list-sdks`

---

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and related workloads.
- **Discover available workloads:** `dotnet workload search maui`
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` as installed.

---

## 4. Install JDK (for Android)

- The MAUI workload installs the **Microsoft OpenJDK** automatically. If you need to install manually:
  - **Source:** [learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) — use **Microsoft Build of OpenJDK 17**.
- **Verify:** `java -version` should show version 17.x.

---

## 5. Install Android SDK & Emulator

The recommended approach is to let the .NET Android workload manage this for you:

```bash
dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net8.0-android \
  -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" \
  -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
```

Run this against any MAUI project (you can create a temporary one — see step 7). Alternatively, install **Android Studio** from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) and use its SDK Manager to install:

- **Android SDK Platform** (API 34 or 35)
- **Android SDK Build-Tools** (latest)
- **Android SDK Command-line Tools**
- **Android Emulator** + a system image

Set the environment variable:
```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
```

**Verify:** `$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --list`

---

## 6. (Optional) Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> Visual Studio for Mac has been retired. VS Code with the **C# Dev Kit** and **.NET MAUI** extensions is now the official free path on macOS.

---

## 7. End-to-End Verification

```bash
# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Run on Android Emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

**Verify each step succeeds with exit code 0.**

---

## Quick Checklist

| Check | Command |
|---|---|
| Xcode installed | `xcodebuild -version` |
| .NET SDK installed | `dotnet --version` |
| MAUI workload installed | `dotnet workload list` |
| JDK available | `java -version` → 17.x |
| Android SDK available | `echo $ANDROID_HOME` is set |
| iOS build works | `dotnet build -f net8.0-ios` |
| Android build works | `dotnet build -f net8.0-android` |

Install in this order: **Xcode → .NET SDK → MAUI workload → Android dependencies → IDE**. Each step depends on the previous one.
